claim.statement: The bounded evaluator does not certify models outside the tested dataset slice.
claim.negation_of: certification of models outside the tested dataset slice

checker.claim_text: The bounded evaluator does not certify models outside the tested dataset slice.

stress_answers.model_degeneracy::Do other models predic: Yes. Two baseline models in dataset slice n=32 could reproduce the same pass/fail observations, so the envelope measures only the release-gate observable.
stress_answers.model_degeneracy::What independent obser: The independent observable is the audit-log count: 0 certifications outside dataset slice n=32, with measured hashes for each evaluated example.
stress_answers.model_degeneracy::How many free paramete: No fitted parameters are used; the gate records 0 learned thresholds and checks the measured dataset slice n=32 against fixed artifact hashes.
stress_answers.model_degeneracy::Has the model been tes: The claim is bounded to the held-out dataset slice n=32; it is not claimed beyond that slice, and independent replication would use a second hashed dataset.
stress_answers.hidden_variable::What variable is not be: The unmeasured variable is distribution shift outside the dataset; the record names it and refuses claims beyond measured slice n=32.
stress_answers.hidden_variable::What assumption may be: The assumption that the dataset slice represents all models may be false; the scope is restricted to measured n=32 artifacts only.
stress_answers.hidden_variable::What would explain the: A contradiction would be explained by an untracked dataset or model version; artifact hashes and measured review counts identify that hidden variable.
stress_answers.hidden_variable::What data would reveal: A second independent dataset slice with n=32 examples and measured artifact hashes would reveal whether the boundary holds beyond the first slice.

provenance.pipeline: complete and adequate answers, but non-human review

one_directional: A non-human review can screen answers but cannot certify correctness.
